title: Exploring icelands glaciers by drone 🧊 #fpvdrone #travelvideographer #iceland
creator: Parker Sheppard (@pshepfpv)
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the glacier/ice/arctic-polar-landscape subgenre bucket. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/rD4_3K2EHP8); per this task's step 1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via `curl "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/rD4_3K2EHP8&format=json"`, which returned title="Exploring icelands glaciers by drone 🧊 #fpvdrone #travelvideographer #iceland", author_name="Parker Sheppard", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@pshepfpv", provider_name="YouTube" -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title/creator claims, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. A WebFetch of the canonical watch-page form (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rD4_3K2EHP8; a first WebFetch attempt on the raw /shorts/ URL returned only a truncated fragment with no usable body content, so the watch-page form was fetched instead) was made specifically to check for a Creative Commons marking, subscriber count, drone model, resolution, or duration; it returned only YouTube's generic footer/navigation and copyright notice ("© 2026 Google LLC") and explicitly found no Creative Commons license, "CC BY" text, subscriber count, drone model, resolution, or duration anywhere in the fetched content. A WebSearch this session ("Parker Sheppard pshepfpv Iceland glaciers drone follower count Instagram TikTok") independently corroborated that this is a real, active, large-following short-form drone creator -- Instagram: 474K followers (instagram.com/pshepfpv), TikTok: 1.1M followers (tiktok.com/@pshepfpv), described as a Salt Lake City-based travel FPV drone pilot whose volcano footage has drawn tens of millions of views, and it separately surfaced a parallel Instagram Reel of the same Iceland-glaciers subject (instagram.com/pshepfpv/reel/C_qdEVDve3B, caption "Glacial views from Iceland") -- consistent with, and corroborating, the prior agent's verification notes. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking was found in this session's WebFetch of the watch page, and per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null despite two separate live-playback attempts to obtain it (a prior agent's, and this session's own -- see `editorial_style.pacing_notes` for the full account): the two attempts returned mutually inconsistent duration readings (31.881s vs. 14.138s) because YouTube Shorts' feed auto-navigates to the next unrelated Short during/after in-page JS execution in both cases, so neither figure can be attributed to this specific clip with confidence -- rather than pick one, this field stays unset pending a real YouTube Data API call (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual FPV creator's hashtag-driven Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's or the prior agent's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.77 and exposure=0.76 sit above this pack's un-curated-personal-account floor (flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.72/0.72, instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's 0.74/0.73) but below its contest-verified top tier (skypixel11_africa_unseen's 0.8/0.8, dronestagram-feature.json's 0.85/0.8) -- there is no confirmed drone model or resolution for this specific clip, but the creator's independently-verified large, genuine following (474K Instagram, 1.1M TikTok) and documented history of viral (tens-of-millions-view) drone footage is a real, disclosed reach/reputation signal above an entirely unvetted personal account, comparable to the Drone Racing League repost exemplar's curation signal (drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json, 0.76/0.74) but arising from the creator's own established audience rather than a third-party curator. motion_smoothness=0.82 is scored at the high end of this pack's range, above sharpness/exposure: FPV genre convention (raw aggressive flight paths smoothed via post-flight gyro-stabilization tools, e.g. ReelSteady/Gyroflow -- the same documented convention cited for drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's 0.80) combined with the creator's demonstrated, independently-corroborated piloting reputation ("acclaimed FPV pilot", tens-of-millions-view volcano footage) as a genre-level, qualitative signal -- not a frame-level motion measurement, which was never performed. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.77, exposure=0.76, motion_smoothness=0.82, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3` in this session; `default_weights()` with no explicit version argument resolved to `weights_version='default-v2'` (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.77+0.76+0.82)/3 = 0.7833333333333333 (rounded to 0.7833 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in multiple independent structural facts: the source URL's own /shorts/ path; the oEmbed response's portrait embed dimensions (height=200, width=113); and this session's own Playwright-based live playback (see below), which loaded the page (Page Title confirmed as "Exploring icelands glaciers by drone 🧊 #fpvdrone #travelvideographer #iceland - YouTube", an exact match to the oEmbed title) and read a real <video> element with videoWidth=720/videoHeight=1280 -- a vertical 9:16 frame, consistent with native-Short format. A prior agent's editorial review pass attempted a genuine live-playback cut-detection pass on this same URL via Playwright: it first confirmed real duration=31.881s and native resolution 720x1280 by direct JS inspection of the <video> element, then attempted to seek through the video at 0.2s intervals diffing downsampled canvas frames to flag hard-cut points, but the page auto-navigated client-side to a different, unrelated Short mid-script, destroying the JS execution context before any diff data was returned; a second attempt, reloading the exact source_url fresh, also failed to hold the target video, redirecting immediately to a different Short. This session independently re-attempted the same live-playback check to corroborate that report: navigating directly to https://www.youtube.com/shorts/rD4_3K2EHP8 (page loaded successfully, title matched exactly, no login/cookie wall), then immediately evaluating `document.querySelector('video')` to read duration/videoWidth/videoHeight -- this returned duration=14.138s and 720x1280, but by the time the evaluate call completed, the page URL had already changed to a different, unrelated Short ("Stunning 4K Drone Footage of City Skyline at Night"), i.e. this session hit the exact same feed-auto-navigation failure mode independently. Critically, this session's duration reading (14.138s) does NOT match the prior agent's (31.881s) -- direct, first-hand evidence that neither figure can be reliably attributed to this specific clip's true runtime, since YouTube Shorts' feed swaps the active <video> element during or immediately after in-page script execution on this URL. The 720x1280 resolution reading was consistent across both attempts and matches the oEmbed portrait-aspect metadata, so the vertical/short-form structural classification is well-corroborated even though the duration figure is not. Given this repeated, now twice-independently-confirmed failure to hold the target video in a scrubbable state, this session stopped rather than retrying further (per the anti-rabbit-holing guidance), consistent with the prior agent's own decision to stop. No genuine observation of cut count, average shot length, or transition types (hard cut/whip pan/speed ramp/etc.) was obtained by either session, so those fields stay null/empty rather than estimated or guessed. Text-provenance search (this session's WebSearch, plus a prior agent's WebSearch/WebFetch on the video's own page, the creator's Instagram cross-post caption, and general press/profile coverage of Parker Sheppard/@pshepfpv) also turned up no description of this specific video's editing pace or cut rhythm -- the Instagram caption was minimal ("Glacial views from Iceland", hashtags only) with no commentary on this clip's edit structure specifically. review_method is set to 'live_playback_review' because genuine live-playback attempts (by both the prior agent and this session, independently) were actually made and did load and inspect a real <video> element on the target page -- not because they yielded reliable cut/duration data, which per the schema's honesty contract they explicitly did not for duration, and did not at all for cut count/shot length/transitions.
